title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Go Client… · Runtime Contract: Transport Abstraction
summary: A reusable Go SDK needs one configured Client, narrow RoundTripper seams, ordered middleware, typed operations, and rotating configuration. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go transport abstraction increment in a Go 1.26 net/http small composable client library boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Transport Abstraction · Theory Transport Abstraction
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model library needs base authority, injected transport, auth, retry, cache, metrics, redaction, and typed operation G-6. Order wrappers, separate per-request state, validate configuration, and keep protocol plumbing private. Competency probe: Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
